Packaging Material

PET Straps

Heavy-duty polyester strapping for demanding industrial packaging loads.

Tensile strength up to 1500kgWidths from 9mm to 32mmExcellent tension retentionManual & pneumatic tool compatible
Specifications

Product Specifications

MaterialPolyester (PET)
Width9mm, 12mm, 15mm, 19mm, 25mm, 32mm
Thickness0.6mm – 1.2mm
Tensile Strength200kg – 1500kg
ElongationLow elongation — excellent tension retention
SurfaceSmooth / Embossed
ColourBlack, Green
GradeManual grade, Pneumatic tool grade
Applications

Where It's Used

  • ✓Steel coil securing for transport
  • ✓Granite and stone slab unitizing for export
  • ✓Heavy construction materials and timber bundling
  • ✓Heavy machinery and equipment crating
  • ✓Export container load securing
  • ✓Ceramic tile palletizing and heavy industrial pallet loads
FAQ

Frequently Asked Questions

When should I use PET straps instead of PP strapping?

PET strapping is the right choice when load weights exceed what PP can reliably handle, typically above 200–300kg per strap. PET retains tension far better than PP under vibration, heat, or load settling — which makes it essential for steel, stone, or heavy industrial loads in transit.

How do I determine the right tensile strength for my application?

As a general guide, the total strap tensile capacity should be at least 1.5–2× the load weight to allow for dynamic forces in transit. For example, a 1,000kg pallet ideally needs straps rated at 1,500kg+ in total. Share your load weight and strap count and we can recommend the right specification.

Are PET straps reusable?

PET straps are generally single-use once tensioned and cut. They should not be re-tensioned after removal as the buckle crimp point becomes a weak spot. However, short recovered pieces can be repurposed for lighter bundling if they are undamaged.

Are your PET straps compatible with pneumatic strapping tools?

Yes. We supply PET straps in widths and grades compatible with standard pneumatic tensioning and sealing tools. Please share your tool brand and model when ordering to ensure we supply the correct width and thickness.

What widths do you recommend for granite or stone loads?

For granite slabs and heavy stone exports, 25mm or 32mm wide PET strapping with high tensile grades (800kg–1500kg) is recommended. The wider strap distributes load better and reduces the risk of cutting into stone edges. Corner protectors are also advised.

Do you supply a high-tensile grade suitable for container export loads?

Yes. We stock high-tensile PET strapping rated for export-grade container loads. These grades have tighter thickness tolerances and consistent elongation specs. Contact us with your container load details for a recommendation.

What are the temperature limitations of PET strapping?

PET strapping performs well between -20°C and +80°C. For high-temperature environments above 80°C (e.g., steelworks, foundry outputs), PET may soften — consult us for alternative options. For normal transport and storage conditions, PET is highly reliable.

What is the minimum order quantity for bulk PET strap orders?

MOQ for PET strapping varies by width and grade. Standard widths (15mm, 19mm) are available in 1-coil quantities for trial orders. Bulk pricing applies from 10 coils upwards. Contact us with your monthly volume for pricing.
